title: Abort: negative `gc_refs` ("refcount too small") in `validate_gc_objects` (`gc_free_threading.c:1116`)
description: Under a dense OOM sweep, `socket.recv_fds` repeatedly fails `array.array("i")` construction; the interaction between the deferred reference `tp_alloc` takes on the `array.array` type and the unconditional `Py_DECREF(tp)` in `array_dealloc` (`arraymodule.c:848`) leaves the **type** object's refcount smaller than the live references to it. At shutdown, the free-threaded cyclic GC's `update_refs`/`visit_decref` tally drives `gc_refs` below zero and the debug assertion `gc_get_refs(op) >= 0` in `validate_gc_objects` aborts.
repro_start: sweep 0..999 (cumulative; crash surfaces at finalization GC, not a single start)
notes: Refcount-balance bug, build-agnostic; only debug GC invariants catch it. The corrupted object is the array.array TYPE (object type name: type, repr <class 'array.array'>), reported ob_refcnt 1152921504606847026 = 0x1000000000000032 = _Py_REF_DEFERRED base + small merged-shared, drifted ~-4 below healthy baseline. Surfaced via socket.recv_fds/send_fds which do `import array; array.array("i")` then recvmsg/sendmsg under OOM; newarrayobject (arraymodule.c:770/782) succeeds tp_alloc (deferred ref on type) then fails PyMem_NEW -> Py_DECREF(op) -> array_dealloc -> Py_DECREF(tp) at L848. Crash is at _Py_Finalize -> PyGC_Collect -> gc_collect_main -> deduce_unreachable_heap -> validate_gc_objects; faulthandler Python stack is empty on the representative (runtime state: finalizing). jit (debug GIL) build aborts at Python/gc.c:96 gc_decref 'gc_get_refs(g) > 0' on the SAME underlying bug; ft_release and upstream compile assertions out (NDEBUG) and exit cleanly (n/a). Minimization DONE (2026-06-19): shrinkray-reduced to a bare stdlib `socket.recv_fds(0,0,0)` OOM sweep (range(259)), no boilerplate -- DISPROVING the earlier alignment-sensitive/needs-baseline claim. Reliable ~75%/run (15/20), rc=134 on ft_debug_asan (PYTHON_GIL=0); re-verified N=50. A standalone `array.array("i")` sweep still does not reproduce (the corrupting path is recv_fds's construct-array-then-fail). dedupe.py: NEW (no match to OOM-0001..0007). Additional vehicles (urllib.request -> _NamedIntConstant, _pyrepl.pager) were mislabeled oomNEW by the pre-hardening dedup, which keyed on the secondary ceval.h:148 check_invalid_reentrancy assert instead of the primary gc_free_threading.c:1116 gc_get_refs>=0 assert; hardened oom_dedup matches both and maps them to OOM-0017. The corrupted object varies by vehicle (array, _NamedIntConstant, ...) -- it's the GC refcount-too-small corruption under OOM.
